title: Counting Belugas From Orbit
concept: NOAA's GAIA machine-vision is being taught to find the endangered Cook Inlet belugas in satellite imagery, a genuinely new way to see the nearly unseeable. It cannot count them yet, and whether it ever can turns on one very Alaskan question, can it get a clear look through a slice of sky the Port of Anchorage and Elmendorf already fill.

angle.thesis: A new orbital eye is learning to find Alaska's rarest whales, and the honest question is whether the sky it needs ever stays open.
supporting_facts: GAIA (NOAA lead, USGS, Microsoft AI for Good, Naval Research Laboratory) pairs very-high-resolution satellite imagery with machine learning and expert human annotation, retrieving imagery via the USGS EarthExplorer API.
Cook Inlet belugas are down to a best estimate of 331 (2022 aerial survey), an ~80% fall from ~1,300 in 1979, though the trend may be stabilizing.
In June 2025, tasking aligned with the aerial survey yielded no imagery, likely due to competing priorities near the Port of Anchorage and JBER.
angle.fair_counterpoint: The tool is experimental (not deployed), the 331 count is an aerial-survey number not an AI result, and the whales may be recovering, so this is a promising unfinished tool, never a failure.
angle.runner_ups: Pure celebration (risked implying deployment) and pure caution (leaned a hedged month into sourness); both grafted in as the opening wonder and the honest turn.

treatment.judge_reasoning: Four convergent treatments; the Explainer wins on the strongest scroll-stop (FIND THE WHALE makes the muted viewer squint), the most REVELATION currency (a moving cutaway pipeline that makes the invisible ML process physical), and the best feasibility in the flat-vector engine. Grafted: the Enthusiast's honest AERIAL-not-me stamp (keeps 331 off the AI) and its button line about the sky letting it look; the Dramatist's confidence-score-as-question-mark recurring motif; the Comedian's booked-sky payoff. Fair by construction: the wonder leads, the honest open question is the earned turn, the possibly-stabilizing population is the counterweight.
treatment.logline: NOAA is teaching a machine to find Alaska's rarest whales in satellite photos, and whether it ever works turns on one very Alaskan question, can it get a clear look through a slice of sky the port and the air base already own.

fingerprint.palette: Cook Inlet pewter-teal silt, orbital blue-black, detection cyan to warning amber, beluga bone-white
fingerprint.metaphor: teaching a computer to find a nearly invisible whale in a muddy satellite pixel, and the one clear slice of sky it needs is already booked

palette: Cook Inlet pewter-teal silt, orbital blue-black, detection cyan to warning amber, beluga bone-white

light.sun: soft, high, diffuse north light (overcast) on the silt water, the satellite key from screen-left
light.mood: patient and difficult, a flat shadowless sky over water that hides its subject, warm amber only where the eye locks on

hook.frame1: Top-down swirling glacial silt water filling the 9:16 frame, a pale beluga smudge barely visible in it, ink-bold FIND THE WHALE burned in from frame 0.
hook.headline: FIND THE WHALE
hook.loopback: The closing shot returns to this same silt and pale smudge with the reticle re-settling, the question now whether the sky it needs ever stays open.

reveals.what: the 331 count gives way to a plunging decline curve from 1,300 (1979) to 279 (2018) then a faint uptick toward recovery
pull back from the inlet water to reveal the whole SatelliteEye in orbit above it, recontextualizing the search as seen from space
the GAIA learning pipeline assembles stage by stage, EarthExplorer retrieval then expert annotation then the ML detector

divergence_note: This diverges hard from the last two dispatches. 07-22 (the checkpoint lever) was a cross-section-cutaway in single-object-void, an editorial-schematic held in a locked-drift night-practical frame. 07-21 (the server) was an eye-level naturalistic small-town scene with a boom-drop reveal and a copper hero. This piece is a top-down-map instrument-readout of glacial-silt water and orbital blue-black, an aerial-descent camera in overcast-diffuse light, hero_treatment data-as-subject (the detection and the count), a radial-emanate scan as the dominant motion. Different POV, layout, motion, hero, register, camera and light, and a detection cyan-to-amber palette neither recent piece used. The hero is the net-new SatelliteEye, not a reused server box or lever.

choreo.reaction: the pale beluga curve undulates faintly
choreo.ambient: marine-snow particles drift on a near plane

beats.shows: the smudge resolving into a pale whale shape

draw.subject: the pale beluga smudge

choreo.reaction: the reticle brackets clamp inward

beats.means: the nearly unseeable becomes findable

beats.shows: a starburst 331 stat badge (as it is spoken)

draw.action: counts up to 331 and lands with a hit

beats.means: the crash and the hedged maybe-recovery

draw.action: the eye pings each partner chip as the pipeline boots up

beats.shows: the NEEDS A CLEAR LOOK STRAIGHT DOWN shaft

draw.action: settles under the held silt image

choreo.reaction: a soft cyan glow pulses once

beats.means: the brand button on the held silt frame

hero.name: Cook Inlet silt water + beluga smudge + reticle
hero.detail_note: layered swirling silt planes (parallax), a pale bone-white beluga curve half-dissolved in murk, a thin cyan reticle, a resolving pixel grid, marine-snow particles on a near plane. The whale is soft-organic and treated with care, never comic.

shots.centered: the pale beluga smudge resolving under the reticle at frame center
shots.means: open on the nearly unseeable subject

hero.name: 331 stat badge + decline curve
hero.detail_note: a form-shaded starburst 331 badge, a labeled decline curve from 1,300 to 279 with a faint uptick, an axis, an honest 2022-aerial-survey tag. Ink outlines, multi-tone fills.

shots.centered: the 331 badge then the plunging curve at center
shots.means: link the invisible whale to how few are left

hero.name: SatelliteEye in orbit
hero.detail_note: net-new SatelliteEye: boxy pewter bus, two indigo solar wings, high-gain dish, blinking lights, the downward glowing imaging eye (the tell), an imaging cone reaching to the inlet curve below. Form-shaded, rim-lit, contact shadow.

shots.centered: the SatelliteEye centered in orbital black, cone reaching down
shots.means: reveal the new eye above the search

hero.name: the GAIA learning pipeline
hero.detail_note: a moving cutaway conveyor: GAIA nameplate + partner chips, an EarthExplorer retrieval hopper, an expert-annotation hand stamping labeled boxes, an ML detector box learning. Fat outlined arrows carry the flow, each stage clicks with a sound.

shots.centered: the conveyor stage under the pointer at each click, center-frame
shots.means: make the invisible ML process physical

hero.name: SatelliteEye straining
hero.detail_note: push in on the SatelliteEye, iris wide then squinting, a faint effort bead (earnest, not comic) (on the satellite, never the whale), brows set, the honest CANNOT COUNT YET / STILL LEARNING labels. The cone narrows to a bright shaft as it tilts straight down.

shots.centered: the straining imaging eye at center
shots.means: the honest limit, the machine still learning

hero.name: the crowded-sky map
hero.detail_note: top-down Cook Inlet, the satellite imaging cone reaching down, overlapping airspace rings labeled PORT OF ANCHORAGE and JBER crowding the same slice tagged LIKELY BOOKED, a JUNE 2025 filmstrip frame stamping empty, a detection box blinking a question mark. The rings and the empty month read as CORRELATION on one shared slice of sky, not a stated mechanical cause (NOAA says 'likely'). Hard rectilinear institutional claims vs the soft water.

shots.centered: the contested slice of sky where cone and rings overlap, center-frame
shots.means: the turn, why the learning stalls

hero.name: the whale in silt (loop) + wordmark
hero.detail_note: loop back to the opening silt water and pale beluga, the reticle re-settling and the eye softening to a patient blink, the closing question and the ALASKA.AI wordmark. Negative space, the whale alone in the gray.

shots.centered: the pale whale smudge re-centered under the settling reticle
shots.means: loop the question back to the open, unresolved
